title: [WIP] ecp5: replace ecp5_ffinit with techmap rules + dff2dffs -match-init
body:
~Driver for this was that I need to do the init-matching before I do `zinit` so that ABC9 can operate on zero-initialised flops.~
A weaker reason would be to get rid of custom C++ passes for things that can be done using `techmap`?

There are a few TODO items that I'm not sure about:
* ~Why is `LSRMUX="INV"` not used?~
* Is is true that ECP5 cannot support FFs with asynchronous set initialised to 0/asynchronous reset initialised to 1?
* When `LSRMODE="PRLD"` (used for latches and SR flops) can it support any initial value? The sim model: https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/blob/7025881a5e8c4feb077d296f996683094e2a313a/techlibs/ecp5/cells_sim.v#L319-L326
indicates that the initial value would be the input `M`... Rather conservatively, I only allow `init=1'bx` here.
